question: I'm researching a Texas county that ranked in the top five for average summer meal program sites from 2018-19, ... 2022-2023. In the year (within 2020-2022) that has the highest total subway ridership in MTA, it also had under 12% of its population aged 65+ and more than 3,000 children served in family preservation services. Who were the two counties named after? Please only report the absolute difference in their birth years in your final answer. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Summing Subways: Total Estimated Ridership by year (from Date) for 2020-2022, the maximum total is 1012505879 in 2022.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2020-2022 has the highest total subway ridership in MTA? (Use Date to derive year; sum Subways: Total Estimated Ridership by year and select the maximum.)

2.fact: In 2018-2019, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1052), DALLAS (1014), HIDALGO (494), BEXAR (411), TARRANT (367).
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2018-2019? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2018-2019'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

3.fact: In 2019-2020, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1919), DALLAS (1479), BEXAR (1038), TARRANT (688), HIDALGO (515).
3.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2019-2020? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2019-2020'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

4.fact: In 2020-2021, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (2674), DALLAS (1504), BEXAR (1168), TARRANT (1033), HIDALGO (700).
4.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2020-2021? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2020-2021'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

5.fact: In 2021-2022, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1825), DALLAS (1545), BEXAR (814), TARRANT (757), HIDALGO (526).
5.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2021-2022? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2021-2022'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

6.fact: In 2022-2023, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1016), DALLAS (688), BEXAR (364), HIDALGO (353), TARRANT (303).
6.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2022-2023? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2022-2023'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

7.fact: In <node_1 answer>, among the top 5 counties by average summer meal sites, Harris (11.2%) and Dallas (11.5%) had less than 12% of their population aged 65 and older, while Bexar (13.1%), Tarrant (13.1%), and Hidalgo (12.3%) had 12% or more.
7.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 2-6 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Tarrant, Hidalgo> had less than 12% of their population aged 65 and older in <node_1 answer>? (Filter: Fiscal Year == <node_1 answer>; use '65 and older: % of Population' < 12.)

8.fact: In <node_1 answer>, among the top 5 counties by average summer meal sites, Harris (3312), Bexar (4612), and Tarrant (3101) had more than 3000 children served in family preservation services, while Dallas (2582) and Hidalgo (882) had fewer.
8.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 2-6 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Tarrant, Hidalgo> had more than 3000 children served in family preservation services in <node_1 answer>? (Filter: Fiscal Year == <node_1 answer>; sum 'Children served in FBSS Family Preservation Services' by County across race/ethnicity; keep > 3000.)

9.fact: According to Wikipedia, Harris County was founded on December 22, 1836 and 'is named for John Richardson Harris, who founded the town of Harrisburg on Buffalo Bayou in 1826.' Its county seat is Houston.
9.subquestion: Who is <intersection of nodes 7-8: Harris County> named after, and what is its county seat?

10.fact: According to Wikipedia, 'The city is named after former General Sam Houston, who was president of the Republic of Texas and had won Texas's independence from Mexico at the Battle of San Jacinto.'
10.subquestion: Who is <node 9 county seat: Houston> named after?

11.fact: According to Wikipedia, 'John Richardson Harris (October 22, 1790 – August 21, 1829) was an American settler of Mexican Texas and the namesake of Harris County, Texas.'
11.subquestion: In what year was <node 9: John Richardson Harris> born?

12.fact: According to Wikipedia, 'Samuel Houston (March 2, 1793 – July 26, 1863) was an American general and statesman who played a prominent role in the Texas Revolution.'
12.subquestion: In what year was <node 10: Sam Houston> born?

reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=1, data.gov year derivation - node 1):
Node 1: Year with highest MTA subway ridership (2020-2022) -> 2022
HOP 2 (d=5, data.gov AGGREGATION - nodes 2-6):
Node 2: Summer Meal 2018-2019 -> top 5 counties by site count (see node 2 answer)
Node 3: Summer Meal 2019-2020 -> top 5 counties by site count (see node 3 answer)
Node 4: Summer Meal 2020-2021 -> top 5 counties by site count (see node 4 answer)
Node 5: Summer Meal 2021-2022 -> top 5 counties by site count (see node 5 answer)
Node 6: Summer Meal 2022-2023 -> top 5 counties by site count (see node 6 answer)
AGGREGATION: Compute average across all 5 years -> Top 5 are {HARRIS: 1697.2, DALLAS: 1246.0, BEXAR: 759.0, TARRANT: 629.6, HIDALGO: 517.6}
Note: All 5 years required because Harris leads overall while other counties shift in rank by year
HOP 3 (d=2, data.gov INTERSECTION - nodes 7-8):
Node 7: From top 5, elderly < 12% in 2022 -> {Harris, Dallas}
Node 8: From top 5, family preservation > 3000 in 2022 -> {Harris, Bexar, Tarrant}
INTERSECTION: {Harris, Dallas} ∩ {Harris, Bexar, Tarrant} = {Harris}
Both criteria necessary: Node 7 alone gives 2 counties, Node 8 alone gives 3 counties
HOP 4 (d=2, Wikipedia - nodes 9-10):
Node 9: Harris County Wikipedia -> named after John Richardson Harris, county seat is Houston
Node 10: Houston Wikipedia -> city named after Sam Houston
Result: Two namesakes identified - John Richardson Harris (county) and Sam Houston (city)
HOP 5 (d=2, Wikipedia AGGREGATION - nodes 11-12):
Node 11: John Richardson Harris Wikipedia -> born October 22, 1790
Node 12: Sam Houston Wikipedia -> born March 2, 1793
AGGREGATION: 1793 - 1790 = 3 years difference
Final answer: 3
